spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates `calc(value / 1px)` for unit stripping, the central spec change vs the old `tan(atan2(...))` workaround.

interactive.rationale: Two interactive markers (slider + readout). Could push more — the old vs new vs derived value side-by-side would benefit from more controls.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb explicitly cites the old tan(atan2) trick; the page contrasts old vs new.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show the trick fail-state (negative em? sub-pixel? zero?) or the precision limit of the trick.

openQuestions.title: Show the old tan(atan2) hack side-by-side
openQuestions.detail: The lede mentions it. Render the value computed via the old trick AND via the new calc, and prove they match across the slider range. Authors will get the migration mental model in one glance.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate zero-divisor and precision behaviour
openQuestions.detail: calc(0px / 1px) = 0 is fine, but the old hack had precision loss near 0. Show the gap on a log-scale slider so the credibility of the new approach lands.

openQuestions.title: Pair with use case (style-queries / line-height ratio)
openQuestions.detail: The unitless ratio is most useful for line-height or scale(). Wire the derived value into a line-height computation so visitors see the workflow, not just the conversion.

summary: Strong motivating demo. Adding the explicit old-vs-new comparison and a real consumer (line-height) would make the migration story complete.
